---
title: "test"
output: html_notebook
---



```{r setup, include=TRUE}
# Load Required Libraries and Import Data
library(openxlsx)   # for reading/writing Excel files
library(dplyr)      # for data manipulation
library(tidyr)      # for reshaping data
library(stringr)    # for string pattern matching

# Define file paths
file_path <- "data/included_studies_1-13.xlsx"           # input file path
output_path <- "output/long_format_with_all_corr.xlsx" # unused output path

# Read the Excel file starting from row 2, assuming headers start there
data <- read.xlsx(file_path, startRow = 2)

# 🔧 Function to clean and standardize column names
clean_names <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub('["\']', "", x)          # Remove quotation marks
  x <- gsub("&#10;?", "", x)         # Remove HTML newline codes
  x <- gsub("[\r\n]", "", x)         # Remove actual newline characters
  x <- trimws(x)                     # Trim leading/trailing spaces
  x <- gsub("\\s+", "", x)           # Remove inner spaces
  return(x)
}

# Apply the cleaning function to column names
names(data) <- clean_names(names(data))

```


```{r static-and-dynamic-columns}
# Define Static Columns and Filter WB Variables


# List of static columns (subject identifiers, authors, year, etc.)
keep_static <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "29_WbPoint")

# Regex patterns to match dynamic WB columns
wb_patterns <- c(
  "^\\d+_WBmt\\d+$",           # e.g. 12_WBmt1
  "^\\d+_WBsdt\\d+$",          # e.g. 12_WBsdt1
  "^\\d+_WBt(\\d+)t(\\d+)$",   # e.g. 12_WBt1t2
  "^\\d+_WBcort(\\d+)t(\\d+)$" # e.g. 12_WBcort1t2
)

# Collect columns that match the WB patterns
wb_cols <- unique(unlist(lapply(wb_patterns, function(p) grep(p, names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE))))


#wb_cols <- wb_cols[sapply(wb_cols)]

# Combine static and dynamic columns
all_cols <- intersect(c(keep_static, wb_cols), names(data))

# Subset the data with selected columns
data <- data[, all_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Convert all non-static (i.e., dynamic WB) columns to numeric
data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)] <- lapply(data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)], as.numeric)

```

```{r Adding time intervals}
# Define Time Intervals
timepoints <- 1:11                       # From timepoint 1 to 11 (for intervals 1-2 to 11-12)
intervals <- paste0(timepoints, "-", timepoints + 1)  # Create interval labels (e.g., "1-2", "2-3", etc.)

# Prepare an empty list to store reshaped rows
long_rows <- list()

```


```{r Transform Each Row into Long Format}

long_rows <- list()
max_t <- 12  # max timepoint

for (i in 1:nrow(data)) {
  static_info <- data[i, keep_static]

  for (t1 in 1:(max_t - 1)) {
    for (t2 in (t1 + 1):max_t) {
      interval_label <- paste0(t1, "-", t2)

      # Find the exact column names
      mt1 <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", t1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)
      sdt1 <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", t1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)
      mt2 <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", t2, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)
      sdt2 <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", t2, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)
      tlag <- grep(paste0("_WBt", t1, "t", t2, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)
      corr <- grep(paste0("_WBcort", t1, "t", t2, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE)

      # Create the row with only relevant fields
      row <- static_info
      row$interval <- interval_label

      for (col in c(mt1, sdt1, mt2, sdt2, tlag, corr)) {
        row[[col]] <- data[[col]][i]
      }

      long_rows[[length(long_rows) + 1]] <- row
    }
  }
}

# Combine all rows
long_data <- bind_rows(long_rows) # Combine all reshaped rows into a single data frame

```


```{r Timepoints processing}
# Add Timepoints and Rename Original Column

long_data <- long_data %>%
  mutate(timepoints = as.numeric(`29_WbPoint`)) %>%  # Convert timepoints to numeric
  select(-`29_WbPoint`)                              # Rename original WB point column


```


```{r Filter Rows Based on Timepoints}

# Add interval start (e.g., for "1-2", interval_start = 1)
long_data$interval_start <- as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", long_data$interval))

# Retain only rows where interval start < max timepoints
long_data <- long_data %>%
  filter(is.na(timepoints) | interval_start < timepoints) %>%
  select(-interval_start)  # Drop temporary column

```

```{r Output}
# Write Final Output to Excel (unstyled)
write.xlsx(long_data, "output/long_format_with_all_corr_type2.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)
cat("Final long-format Excel written successfully with original column names intact.")

```
